purpose: Reusable syntax-zero templates distilled from EVAS/Spectre mismatches and the R26 remaining-10 spectrefix closure. These templates are simulator-compatibility constraints, not task-specific circuit answers.

templates.scope: All Verilog-A modules intended for real Spectre validation.

failure_signature: PWL wave bracket with backslash continuation
templates.scope: Spectre testbenches with generated PWL voltage sources.
Generated analog blocks with local real declarations inside runtime event bodies.

failure_signature: parameter value exceeds upper bound in from range limit
templates.scope: Parameters whose legal default lies exactly on an excluded open range endpoint.

failure_signature: Spectre testbench generated Verilog-style multiline instance
templates.good_pattern: XDUT (clk_in rst_n code_3 code_2 code_1 code_0) ramp_gen

templates.bad_pattern: XDUT (...) bg_cal navg=1 with no navg parameter in module
templates.scope: Generated DUT/testbench pairs where the testbench passes a parameter that the DUT module does not declare.

failure_signature: ramp checker exposes code non-monotonicity after testbench begins driving clock
binary counter glitches fail monotonic decoded-code check
templates.bad_pattern: code = code + 1; drive code bits as binary
templates.good_pattern: on each clock edge, latch one additional output bit high: 0, 1, 3, 7, 15
templates.scope: Ramp-generator tasks whose prompt/checker expects monotone thermometer expansion.
